name: North Hills Golf Academy
about: North Hills Golf Academy is a family-oriented golf training studio located in the greater Pittsburgh area, with an indoor facility in Gibsonia and partnerships with outdoor golf courses. They offer year-round instruction, including group lessons for women and juniors, and private lessons. The academy emphasizes a family-oriented approach and has been providing instruction for over 20 years, with current owners who were once junior golfers in the program. Instruction covers fundamentals like putting, chipping, and full swing, with group lessons typically running four to five weeks and requiring participants to bring their own golf clubs.
why_golfers_like_it: Year-round indoor instruction facility in Gibsonia
Partnerships with outdoor golf courses for on-course training
Specialized group programs for women and junior golfers
Experienced coaches with a 20-year history in the Pittsburgh area

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of food service on-site
No mention of alcohol service on-site

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, all participants are required to bring their own set of golf clubs to lessons and group classes.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not mention food or drink service, suggesting it's BYOB (Bring Your Own) if permitted, but this isn't explicitly confirmed.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole simulation take?
faq.answer: The website does not provide information on simulator capabilities or 18-hole simulation times, as the academy focuses on instruction rather than full course play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the academy is family-oriented and offers specialized junior golf programs, making it kid-friendly with appropriate supervision guidelines for indoor and outdoor lessons.
